compute.cost_note: stdlib + Tier 1-3 variance signals; CPU only, no model loads, no API costs

do_not_use_when: You want a single robustness or accuracy number — this surface refuses any aggregate score
You want a provenance verdict for a document — it is not a detector
You have not brought fixture pairs yet (real PAN data arrives via a PAN-account-gated, local-only fetcher)

examples.description: Replay all classes present in a fixture dir
Restrict to the unicode and short classes

inputs.target: fixture directory containing a pairs.jsonl manifest of (clean, obfuscated) text pairs

purpose: Replay SETEC's existing signals over PAN@CLEF obfuscation fixtures (unicode / paraphrase / lang_switch / short) and emit a per-(signal × obfuscation-class) robustness card: relative change plus a stable / degraded / collapsed tag per cell. Reuses the validation harness's audit_text + classify_compression scoring and adversarial_robustness_card's output shape. Not a detector; emits no aggregate robustness or accuracy score.
use_when: You have (clean, obfuscated) PAN fixture pairs and want to know which signals survive which obfuscation class
You're prioritizing which SETEC signals need hardening against tokenizer / paraphrase / code-switch / short-text attacks
You want an honesty-claim artifact ("signal S degrades by Δ under class C") rather than a robustness leaderboard
